data.title: Add documentation discipline rule to CLAUDE.md and agents.md

data.body:
Reviewed cold against plan acceptance criteria.

AC1: PASS - '### Documenting Shipped Work' present in CLAUDE.md at line 178, between 'Where Learnings Go' (174) and 'Quick Reference' (194).
AC2: PASS - same section in agents.md at line 176, same placement (172/192).
AC3: PASS - diff <(sed -n '/^## Lattice/,$p' CLAUDE.md) <(sed -n '/^## Lattice/,$p' agents.md) is empty.
AC4: PASS - 'git show 7dad98f --stat' shows only +16/+16 in those two files; no other content touched.
AC5: PASS - commit msg 'docs(lattice): require user-facing doc updates as a review-gate condition' matches the requested form exactly.

Quality: rule reads cleanly. The 'Document / Don't document' split is concrete and actionable (endpoints/env vars/decision rationale vs. impl details/git history/speculative work). The closing line ('if a shipped feature isn't there, it effectively doesn't exist for them') gives the rule a memorable hook.

Teeth observation (non-blocking, as plan flagged): the rule asserts the review gate enforces it, but the existing '### The Review Gate' 5-step list at line 88 was deliberately not updated. In practice, whether this rule bites depends on review sub-agents reading the whole Lattice section, not just the gate's checklist. For paragraph-density CLAUDE.md docs that's a reasonable bet, but if doc-update misses start slipping through review, the next iteration should add an explicit step 5 ('check user-facing doc') to the Review Gate list. Documented as out-of-scope by the plan; calling it out so the next agent has the breadcrumb.

acceptance criteria met
